prompt: Why are terminal-agent benchmarks useful for long-horizon RL research?
reference_answer: Terminal-agent benchmarks are useful for long-horizon RL because they place agents in executable terminal environments where tasks can involve compiling code, setting up servers, or other end-to-end workflows. Terminal-Bench provides a reproducible task suite and execution harness, while Terminal-Bench-RL shows how such environments can be connected to scalable RL infrastructure for terminal and coding agents. The key value is verifiable task completion over long trajectories rather than short text-only answers.

documents.title: Terminal-Bench repository digest
documents.content: Terminal-Bench tests AI agents in real terminal environments on complicated end-to-end tasks such as compiling code, training models, and setting up servers. It includes a task dataset and an execution harness, and each task can include instructions, tests, and oracle solutions.

documents.title: Terminal-Bench website digest
documents.content: Terminal-Bench is presented as a benchmark for AI agents in terminal environments, with public task examples and leaderboards for measuring terminal mastery and task-resolution success rates.

documents.title: Terminal-Bench-RL digest
documents.content: Terminal-Bench-RL focuses on training long-horizon terminal and coding agents with reinforcement learning. The project builds on rLLM and describes infrastructure for scaling RL training on terminal-based task completion.

required_claims.text: Terminal-agent benchmarks are useful because they evaluate agents in executable terminal environments with end-to-end tasks.
Terminal-Bench provides a reproducible task dataset and execution harness with tests or oracle solutions.
Terminal-Bench-RL connects terminal task completion to scalable reinforcement learning infrastructure for long-horizon agents.

provenance.refresh_hint: Refresh if Terminal-Bench task definitions, harness structure, or Terminal-Bench-RL training claims change.
notes: Focused on terminal-environment and long-horizon RL benchmarks.
